name: Nebhi et al. Acceptable LLM Annotation Benchmark Extract
population_or_context: Adaptive English writing assessment for the EF Standard English Test pipeline.
sample_size: Approximately 200 human-rated gold-standard item-response pairs for annotator benchmarking; 20,000 synthetic response pairs for response-generator fine-tuning; approximately 200 synthetic answers per new prompt in scoring-model integration.
methods: Extracted expert-human annotation, LLM annotator benchmarking, majority-vote annotation, synthetic response generation, and scoring-model integration.
education_context: Adaptive English writing assessment with proficiency-aligned writing prompts and grammatical accuracy ratings.
response_origin: Synthetic writing responses are generated to emulate authentic student work across proficiency levels and are annotated for grammatical accuracy and proficiency level.
rubric_label_space: Grammatical accuracy scale from 0 to 4 and proficiency-aligned writing levels.
label_source: Five expert human raters establish a gold-standard benchmark; top-performing LLM annotators are selected by Cohen kappa against that benchmark and combined by majority vote.
generation_model: A fine-tuned LLaMA 3.1 8B model generates synthetic responses after ORPO fine-tuning; annotation candidates include LLaMA 3.3 70B, GPT-4o, Nova Pro, Mistral models, and Claude Opus.
generation_method: Generate proficiency-aligned synthetic responses for validated writing items, annotate response pairs with an LLM ensemble, and use the data to fine-tune response generation and scoring components.
prompt_strategy: Few-shot item generation and proficiency-conditioned response generation are used; exact prompts are not treated as a reusable scoring standard.
evaluation_method: Candidate LLM annotators are compared to expert human ratings with Cohen kappa; majority-vote annotation is then used for larger synthetic response annotation.
scorer_use: Potentially acceptable for scoring-model development when model annotators are benchmarked against expert human labels and limitations are explicit.
real_response_comparator: A human-rated gold standard of real item-response pairs benchmarks LLM annotation before synthetic response annotation is used at scale.
reported_metrics: Cohen kappa values include LLaMA 3.3 70B at 0.86, GPT-4o at 0.87, Nova Pro at 0.78, and the selected ensemble at 0.89 against the human-annotated gold standard.
limitations: Majority-vote LLM annotation is not equivalent to independent human scoring of all generated responses.
The domain is English writing assessment and grammatical accuracy, not all constructed-response scoring.
